sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.109 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A skew brace is a set$B$equipped with two operations$+$and$$such that$(B,+)$is an additively written (but not necessarily abelian) group,$(B, )$is a multiplicatively written group, and$a (b+c)=ab-a+ac$for any$a,b,c B$. Is there a finite skew brace with perfect additive group and almost simple multiplicative group? { C.\,Tsang}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
